# UC-20.1.12 · FinOps Budget Alert Correlation

## Description

Joins AWS Budgets / Azure budget notifications with resource change events and anomaly searches to explain **why** a threshold fired.

## Value

Joins AWS Budgets / Azure budget notifications with resource change events and anomaly searches to explain **why** a threshold fired.

## Implementation

Ingest budget notifications via HEC or Lambda. Drill down to service cost change same day. Link to change tickets.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cloud billing TAs, CloudTrail.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Budget alert SNS/email logs, `cost:daily`, CloudTrail.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest budget notifications via HEC or Lambda. Drill down to service cost change same day. Link to change tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=finops sourcetype="aws:budget:alert" earliest=-7d
| eval budget_name=coalesce(budget_name,BudgetName)
| join type=left max=1 budget_name [ search index=cloud_cost sourcetype="cost:daily" earliest=-7d | bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(cost) as daily_cost by account_id, _time ]
| table _time, budget_name, threshold_type, daily_cost
```

Understanding this SPL

**FinOps Budget Alert Correlation** — Joins AWS Budgets / Azure budget notifications with resource change events and anomaly searches to explain **why** a threshold fired.

Documented **Data sources**: Budget alert SNS/email logs, `cost:daily`, CloudTrail. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cloud billing TAs, CloudTrail. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: finops; **sourcetype**: aws:budget:alert. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=finops, sourcetype="aws:budget:alert", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **budget_name** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Pipeline stage (see **FinOps Budget Alert Correlation**): table _time, budget_name, threshold_type, daily_cost


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (budget alerts overlaid with spend), Table (alert + cost delta), Sankey (alert → service).

## SPL

```spl
index=finops sourcetype="aws:budget:alert" earliest=-7d
| eval budget_name=coalesce(budget_name,BudgetName)
| join type=left max=1 budget_name [ search index=cloud_cost sourcetype="cost:daily" earliest=-7d | bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(cost) as daily_cost by account_id, _time ]
| table _time, budget_name, threshold_type, daily_cost
```

## Visualization

Timeline (budget alerts overlaid with spend), Table (alert + cost delta), Sankey (alert → service).
